preamble: The shift from an aspirated alveolar stop to an alveolar fricative represents a process of assibilation and spirantization, often occurring as a later stage in a more complex sound change like the High German Consonant Shift.

examples.note: Development of t > tʰ > ts > s, comparing English 'water' to German 'wasser'.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Languages: A Historical Introduction. Cambridge University Press.
